session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Session-Based Plan Deduplication 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding duplicate plan prevention" 5→ - "working with exit-plan-mode hook" 6→ - "debugging duplicate issue creation" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "modifying marker deletion behavior in exit-plan-mode hook" 9→ warning: "Reusable markers (plan-saved) must persist; one-time markers (implement-now, objective-context) are consumed. Deleting reusable markers breaks state machines and enables retry loops that create duplicates." 10→ - action: "using session-scoped markers in exec scripts" 11→ warning: "Session markers enable idempotency in command retries. Always write markers AFTER successful operation completion, never before. Use triple-check guard on marker read: file exists AND content is valid AND expected type (numeric for issue numbers)." 12→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 13→audit_result: clean 14→--- 15→ 16→# Session-Based Plan Deduplication 17→ 18→This document explains how erk prevents duplicate plan issues from being created during retry loops or hook edge cases. 19→ 20→## Problem
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: This document explains how erk prevents duplicate plan issues from being created during retry loops or hook edge cases.
This document explains how erk prevents duplicate plans from being created during retry loops or hook edge cases.

session.assistant: Now let me fix the learn-workflow.md file (the biggest one with ~10 instances):

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: **Key change**: Learn no longer writes documentation directly. Instead, it creates a plan ...[truncated 94 chars]...s control over documentation quality while maintaining the unified implementation pattern.

**Key change**: Learn no longer writes documentation directly. Instead, it creates a plan ...[truncated 88 chars]...s control over documentation quality while maintaining the unified implementation pattern.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: - `learned_from_issue`: Backlink to the parent plan issue number
- `learned_from_issue`: Backlink to the parent plan number

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: 3. Creates a learn plan issue (if documentation needed) 4. The plan issue is queued for human review before implementation
3. Creates a learn plan (if documentation needed) 4. The plan is queued for human review before implementation

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: **Note**: Learn runs inline during the `plan-implement` workflow after successful implemen...[truncated 1 chars]...rite documentation directly - it creates a plan issue for later review and implementation.
**Note**: Learn runs inline during the `plan-implement` workflow after successful implementation. It does NOT write documentation directly - it creates a plan for later review and implementation.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
After the learn plan issue is created, a human reviews it and decides whether to implement:
1. Review the plan issue - it contains draft content starters and documentation suggestions

After the learn plan is created, a human reviews it and decides whether to implement:
1. Review the plan - it contains draft content starters and documentation suggestions

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: Clicking the cell opens the learn plan issue or PR.
Clicking the cell opens the learn plan or PR.

session.tool_result:
56→ 57→### Parent Plan 58→ 59→The original implementation plan that `/erk:learn` is invoked on. After learn completes, the parent plan's metadata is updated with: 60→ 61→- `learn_status`: Status of the learning workflow 62→- `learn_plan_issue`: Issue number of the created learn plan (if any) 63→- `learn_plan_pr`: PR number that implemented the learn plan (after landing) 64→ 65→### Learn Plan

Now let me also check lines around 160-163 to see the `learn_plan_issue` reference in the code block:
session.tool_result:
155→ 156→```bash 157→erk exec track-learn-result \ 158→ --issue <parent-issue-number> \ 159→ --status completed_with_plan \ 160→ --learn-plan <learn-plan-number> 161→``` 162→ 163→This sets `learn_status` and `learn_plan_issue` on the parent plan. 164→

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: Erk uses a two-label scheme for plan issues: a **base label** for universal identification and a **type-specific label** for filtering and routing.
Erk uses a two-label scheme for plans: a **base label** for universal identification and a **type-specific label** for filtering and routing.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: | `erk-plan` | Type label — implementation plans | Regular plan issues | | `erk-learn` | Type label — documentation plans | Learn plan issues |
| `erk-plan` | Type label — implementation plans | Regular plans | | `erk-learn` | Type label — documentation plans | Learn plans |

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: - "working with older erk-plan issues that lack metadata blocks"
- "working with older erk-plans that lack metadata blocks"

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: Plan issues use a two-location storage design with backward-compatible fallback. Understan...[truncated 15 chars]...s prevents agents from looking in the wrong place and reporting false "no content" errors.

Plans use a two-location storage design with backward-compatible fallback. Understanding w...[truncated 9 chars]...s prevents agents from looking in the wrong place and reporting false "no content" errors.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: Schema v2 plan issues split content across two GitHub API objects for a deliberate reason: **fast querying vs. full content**.
Schema v2 plans split content across two GitHub API objects for a deliberate reason: **fast querying vs. full content**.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: | **v2 metadata** | First comment | `<!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-body -->` with `<details>` | Plan issues |
| **v2 metadata** | First comment | `<!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-body -->` with `<details>` | Plans |

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: **Assuming `plan_comment_id` is always set** — Older issues may not have this field in the...[truncated 17 chars]... back to fetching the first comment via the GitHub API (e.g., `gh issue view --comments`).

**Assuming `plan_comment_id` is always set** — Older plans may not have this field in the ...[truncated 13 chars]...all back to fetching the first comment via the GitHub API (e.g., `gh pr view --comments`).

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: warning: "Planned PR plan_id IS the PR number (not an issue number). Check provider type before assuming plan_id semantics. Issue-based plans use issue numbers; planned-PR plans use PR numbers."
warning: "Planned PR plan_id IS the PR number (not an issue number). Check provider type before assuming plan_id semantics."

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: Planned PR titles (and issue-based plan titles) are prefixed with a label-based tag via `get_title_tag_from_labels()` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_utils.py:178-190`.
Planned PR titles are prefixed with a label-based tag via `get_title_tag_from_labels()` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_utils.py:178-190`.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: When `erk exec plan-save --format json` detects that a plan was already saved in the curre...[truncated 47 chars]...ed_duplicate": true, "plan_number": <N>}`. This JSON is written to **stderr**, not stdout.

When `erk exec plan-save --format json` detects that a plan was already saved in the curre...[truncated 41 chars]...ed_duplicate": true, "plan_number": <N>}`. This JSON is written to **stderr**, not stdout.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: The plan backend is hardcoded to `"planned_pr"`. All plan creation routes through PlannedP...[truncated 90 chars]...11 node 1.1). There is no longer a `"github"` issue-based plan storage path for new plans.

The plan backend is hardcoded to `"planned_pr"`. All plan creation routes through PlannedP...[truncated 13 chars]...end selection via `get_plan_backend()` was removed in PR #7971 (objective #7911 node 1.1).

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

docs/learned/planning/plan-execution-patterns.md:29:1. `erk br co --for-plan <issue-number>` creates a new worktree from the plan issue docs/learned/planning/no-changes-handling.md:30:4. **Posts issue comment**: Notifies the plan issue with a link to the PR docs/learned/planning/no-changes-handling.md:55:2. **If already done**: Close both the PR and the linked plan issue docs/learned/planning/cross-artifact-analysis.md:21:| Linked | PR explicitly references plan issue | PR body contains `Plan: #<issue>` reference | docs/learned/planning/plan-schema.md:4: - "understanding plan issue structure" docs/learned/planning/plan-schema.md:15:Plan issues use a two-part structure optimized for GitHub API performance: docs/learned/planning/learn-vs-implementation-plans.md:92:| `completed_with_plan` | Learn ran, plan issue created | `/erk:learn` (saved plan issue) | docs/learned/planning/learn-vs-implementation-plans.md:102:**Running `/erk:learn` on a learn plan issue**: Creates a documentation cycle. The learn command rejects this with a clear error. docs/learned/planning/plan-header-privatization.md:14:The `plan_header.py` module contains low-level functions for reading/writing plan issue metadata blocks. These are being gradually privatized behind the `PlanBackend` abstraction. docs/learned/planning/learn-pipeline-workflow.md:27:The learn pipeline has 7 stages that transform raw session JSONL into a documentation plan issue. The key architectural decision is **where processing happens**: stages 1-5 run locally (fast, free), while stages 6-7 run in GitHub Actions (background, billed). docs/learned/planning/learn-pipeline-workflow.md:60:Finds all session logs related to a plan issue by querying GitHub issue metadata for tracked session IDs, then resolving those IDs to local file paths. Three session source types exist: docs/learned/planning/learn-pipeline-workflow.md:118:Human reviews the learn plan issue, optionally edits it, and submits for implementation through the standard `plan-implement` workflow. docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md:13: - "debugging missing branch_name in plan issues" docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md:14: - "implementing PR lookup from plan issues" docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md:32:When code needs to look up a PR from a plan issue, `branch_name` may be missing from metadata. The system uses two layers: docs/learned/planning/learn-workflow.md:320:- `issue_number`: Plan issue number docs/learned/planning/learn-workflow.md:333:- `plan_title`: Title from plan issue docs/learned/planning/learn-workflow.md:350:- `plan_title`: Title from plan issue docs/learned/planning/learn-workflow.md:365:- `plan_title`: Title from plan issue docs/learned/planning/tripwires.md:95:**closing a plan issue without verifying all items were addressed** → Read [Complete File Inventory Protocol](complete-inventory-protocol.md) first. Compare the file inventory against the plan's items before closing. Silent omissions are the most common failure mode. docs/learned/planning/tripwires.md:161:**manually creating an erk-plan issue with gh issue create** → Read [Plan Lifecycle](lifecycle.md) first. Use `erk exec plan-save --plan-file <path>` instead. Manual creation requires complex metadata block format (see Metadata Block Reference section). docs/learned/planning/async-learn-local-preprocessing.md:53:**Graceful degradation**: If no PR exists for the plan issue, comment fetching is skipped entirely. Missing PR comments don't block the learn workflow — sessions alone contain sufficient material for insight extraction. docs/learned/planning/workflow-markers.md:35:### Plan Issue Tracking docs/learned/planning/token-optimization-patterns.md:40:The `/erk:replan` command demonstrates this pattern. Step 3 explicitly skips content fetching ("Plan content is fetched by each Explore agent in Step 4, not in the main context"), and Step 4 launches parallel Explore agents that each fetch their own plan issue as their first action. docs/learned/planning/cross-repo-plans.md:11:Store plan issues in a dedicated repository while implementing in another. docs/learned/planning/cross-repo-plans.md:15:- **Private plans**: Keep plan issues in a private repo while implementing in a public repo docs/learned/planning/cross-repo-plans.md:28:Where `owner/plans-repo` is the GitHub repository that will hold plan issues. docs/learned/planning/pr-discovery.md:6: - "finding the PR associated with an erk plan issue" docs/learned/planning/pr-discovery.md:19:Finding the PR associated with a plan issue is a cross-cutting concern that spans multiple commands (`get-pr-for-plan`, `trigger-async-learn`, `erk pr co`, `erk pr close`). Two fundamentally different strategies exist, chosen based on what metadata is available. docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:39:GitHub Actions workflow run URL that created this plan. Provides a backlink from the plan issue to the workflow that generated it.
... [16 lines omitted] ...
docs/learned/planning/plan-backend-migration.md:18:When exec scripts need to read or update plan issues, use `PlanBackend` instead of direct `GitHubIssues` calls. PlanBackend provides typed abstractions with discriminated union error handling. docs/learned/planning/index.md:35:- **[metadata-block-fallback.md](metadata-block-fallback.md)** — extracting plan content from GitHub issue comments, debugging 'no plan content found' errors in replan or plan-implement, working with older erk-plan issues that lack metadata blocks docs/learned/planning/index.md:50:- **[plan-schema.md](plan-schema.md)** — understanding plan issue structure, debugging plan validation errors, working with plan-header or plan-body blocks

session.tool_result:
## Results Report: make fast-ci
**Status: FAILED** - Exit code 2
**Summary:** - Prettier formatting check failed - All other checks passed
**Issues Found:**
### Prettier Formatting (3 files need formatting): 1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md` 3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md`
**Detailed Breakdown:**
